comments.body: Similar question: Getting "a java agent has been loaded" warning in intelliJ after upgrading JDK 17 to 21. Does passing -XX:+EnableDynamicAgentLoading work for you? What about this answer?

Add the option -XX:+EnabledDynamicAgentLoading... that will solve the point... furthermore remove the manually call of the javaagent... check this: blog.soebes.io/posts/2023/10/… (mockito will do it on it's own)...

I added the argument -XX:+EnabledDynamicAgentLoading and the number of warnings were reduced but the first line will not go away Mockito is currently self-attaching to enable the inline-mock-maker.. Can someone help me please?
Note that as of 2025-03-27, the link to the Mockito instructions as given in the warning isn't working. This updated link works: javadoc.io/doc/org.mockito/mockito-core/latest/org.mockito/o‌​rg/…

answers.body: seems like byteagent is not recommended that is my understanding from the error message. Try to explicitly attach using jvm arguments while running the test case.

comments.body: I added the above XML snippet to my pom.xml and added the argument -XX:+EnableDynamicAgentLoading to the VM parameters. All warnings were cleared except the first line Mockito is currently self-attaching to enable the inline-mock-maker... The XML snippets didn't have any effect. Any help would be appreciated.

@tarekahf This works for Maven and IntelliJ IDEA, I haven't tested it in Eclipse, but your answer is essentially the same, so that should work as well. There is no need to use -XX:+EnabledDynamicAgentLoading or the maven-dependency-plugin.
I figured out the root cause of the problem. I have submitted an answer.
I assume this is needed for the Failsafe plugin as well (for integration tests)? Surely they would both require the same configuration, but no one seems to have mentioned it.
What is ${settings.localRepository} in the answer?  How do we set this up, or do we replace it with something?   What is a local Repostiory?

@JohnLittle ${settings.localRepository} refers to the path of your local Maven repository, where Maven stores downloaded dependencies and plugins. You typically don’t need to configure this manually as Maven provides it automatically, so you can safely use it in your pom.xml. By default, it points to ${user.home}/.m2/repository. For more details, see the official Maven Settings Reference
For anyone whose ${settings.localRepository} contains whitespace (ex. space character) you can surround the entire string to the right of -javaagent: with double quotes

answers.body:
Finally fixed, both in command line and IDE (IntelliJ IDEA). There are two different warnings, so the fix comes in two parts, you can try each argument on its own to see the effect:

<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
    <configuration>
        <argLine>
            -javaagent:${settings.localRepository}/org/mockito/mockito-core/${mockito.version}/mockito-core-${mockito.version}.jar
            -Xshare:off
        </argLine>
    </configuration>
</plugin>

No need for -XX:+EnabledDynamicAgentLoading or maven-dependency-plugin.

All the credit goes to this and this answers.

Also, take a look at this to understand why some configuration might only work for the build tool or the IDE. So you should use a neutral configuration, otherwise you will need a different configuration for each.

If you will run the test from Eclipse, you need to change the JUnit Run Configuration.

I added the following to Eclipse JUnit Run Configuration VM Arguments:

-Xshare:off
-javaagent:${env_var:userprofile}\.m2\repository\org\mockito\mockito-core\5.14.2\mockito-core-5.14.2.jar

Change the path to match the location of the jar file on your local.

And all warnings were cleared with the test execution.

Below are the configurations I used in pom.xml:

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>properties</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.5.2</version>
                <configuration>
                    <argLine>
                        -javaagent:${org.mockito:mockito-core:jar}
                        -Xshare:off
                    </argLine>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

Note that I removed @{argLine} as it was causing the Maven Test goal to break. I didn't figure out why using @{argLine} causes the Maven Test build to fail.

I should have known that Maven and Eclipse are different animals that are not 100% integrated.

comments.body: Amazing idea! I went a bit more lazy and made it work for Android too: stackoverflow.com/a/79784066/253468

answers.body:
For gradle projects you can try this. Tested on Intellij only.

test {
    jvmArgs "-javaagent:${configurations.testRuntimeClasspath.find { it.name.contains('mockito-core') }}", '-Xshare:off'
    ...
}

comments.body: you could improve your answers in future with a little more explanation and relevant links etc
I don't think you are using Apache Maven 4.0.0, since it hasn't yet been released. You may, however, be using Apache Maven POM version 4.0.0 (which is what everybody using Maven is de-facto using)

answers.body:
This may help someone using gradle for their projects.

Deriving from what is mentioned in the official docs of mockito-core:

  1. We create a separate configuration mockitoAgent

    configurations {
    .....

    mockitoAgent {
        transitive = false
    }
}

  2. Add mockito-core library to this configuration

    dependencies {
    .....

    mockitoAgent 'org.mockito:mockito-core'
}

  3. Use your mockitoAgent configuration's path (or alternatively, iterate files from testRuntimeClasspath) in the jvmArgs of the gradle test task.

    tasks.named('test') {
    useJUnitPlatform()
    .....

    jvmArgs "-javaagent:${configurations.mockitoAgent.asPath}", "-Xshare:off"
}

comments.body: gradle groovy had to look like this to work for me: configurations { mockitoAgent {} }; dependencies {mockitoAgent platform('org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-dependencies:‌​3.4.4'); mockitoAgent("org.mockito:mockito-core") { transitive = false }; }; tasks.withType(Test).configureEach { doFirst { jvmArgs('-Xshare:off', "-javaagent:${configurations.mockitoAgent.asPath}") } }

answers.body:
If using Spring Boot (and maybe its initializer), there's an issue about it in the initializr repository.

The solution as a contributor pointed out is:

For Maven

 <plugin>
     <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
     <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
     <executions>
         <execution>
             <goals>
                 <goal>properties</goal>
             </goals>
         </execution>
     </executions>
 </plugin>
 <plugin>
     <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
     <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
     <configuration>
         <argLine>@{argLine} -javaagent:${org.mockito:mockito-core:jar}</argLine>
     </configuration>
 </plugin>

For Gradle (Groovy)

def mockitoAgent = configurations.create("mockitoAgent")

dependencies {
     // ...
     mockitoAgent("org.mockito:mockito-core") { isTransitive = false }
}

tasks.named("test") {
     // ...
     jvmArgs("-javaagent:${mockitoAgent.asPath}")
}

For Gradle (Kotlin)

val mockitoAgent = configurations.create("mockitoAgent")

dependencies {
     // ...
     mockitoAgent("org.mockito:mockito-core") { isTransitive = false }
}

tasks.withType<Test> {
     // ...
     jvmArgs("-javaagent:${mockitoAgent.asPath}")
}

Notice no version is specified so spring-boot-starter-test decides which one to use

As most voted answer when writing this comments, beware if the IDE doesn't use your build tool, the configuration won't be applied hence the warning will keep appearing as pointed out in another answer. For instance, I run tests with IntelliJ IDEA + Gradle (it's the default when writing this) so the configuration is applied. Checkout "Run tests using" in Gradle settings to change this behavior in IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate.

Following the idea from crimson589, but using lazy constructs, so it's faster when not testing and hopefully more configuration cache friendly. Also only the Test task as a dependency (no Project.configurations), which means this should work for Android and other systems too, not just org.gradle.java projects.

tasks.withType<Test>().configureEach {
    val mockito = this.classpath.elements.map { files ->
        files.single { it.asFile.name.startsWith("mockito-core-") }
    }
    this.jvmArgumentProviders.add { listOf("-javaagent:${mockito.get().asFile}") }
}

Warning #1:

Mockito is currently self-attaching to enable the inline-mock-maker. This will no longer work in future releases of the JDK. Please add Mockito as an agent to your build what is described in Mockito's documentation: https://javadoc.io/doc/org.mockito/mockito-core/latest/org/mockito/Mockito.html#0.3

https://rieckpil.de/how-to-configure-mockito-agent-for-java-21-without-warning/

Warning #2:


OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM warning: Sharing is only supported for boot loader classes because bootstrap classpath has been appended

https://github.com/mockito/mockito/issues/3111

Solution:

pom.xml

<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>${maven-surefire-plugin.version}</version>
    <configuration>
        <!-- Force alphabetical order to have a reproducible build -->
        <runOrder>alphabetical</runOrder>
        <excludes>
            <exclude>**/*IT*</exclude>
            <exclude>**/*IntTest*</exclude>
        </excludes>
        <argLine>@{argLine} -Xshare:off -javaagent:${settings.localRepository}/org/mockito/mockito-core/${mockito.version}/mockito-core-${mockito.version}.jar</argLine>
    </configuration>
</plugin>

<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>${maven-failsafe-plugin.version}</version>
    <configuration>
        <!-- Due to spring-boot repackage, without adding this property test classes are not found
             See https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/issues/6254 -->
        <!-- Force alphabetical order to have a reproducible build -->
        <classesDirectory>${project.build.outputDirectory}</classesDirectory>
        <runOrder>alphabetical</runOrder>
        <includes>
            <include>**/*IT*</include>
            <include>**/*IntTest*</include>
        </includes>
        <argLine>@{argLine} -Dspring.profiles.active=${profile.test} -Xshare:off -javaagent:${settings.localRepository}/org/mockito/mockito-core/${mockito.version}/mockito-core-${mockito.version}.jar</argLine>
    </configuration>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <id>integration-test</id>
            <goals>
                <goal>integration-test</goal>
            </goals>
        </execution>
        <execution>
            <id>verify</id>
            <goals>
                <goal>verify</goal>
            </goals>
        </execution>
    </executions>
</plugin>

title: Mockito is currently self-attaching to enable the inline-mock-maker. This will no longer work in future releases of the JDK
body:
I get this warning while testing in Spring Boot:

Mockito is currently self-attaching to enable the inline-mock-maker. This will no longer work in future releases of the JDK. Please add Mockito as an agent to your build what is described in Mockito's documentation: https://javadoc.io/doc/org.mockito/mockito-core/latest/org/mockito/Mockito.html#0.3
WARNING: A Java agent has been loaded dynamically (/Users/lcnicolau/.m2/repository/net/bytebuddy/byte-buddy-agent/1.15.10/byte-buddy-agent-1.15.10.jar)
WARNING: If a serviceability tool is in use, please run with -XX:+EnableDynamicAgentLoading to hide this warning
WARNING: If a serviceability tool is not in use, please run with -Djdk.instrument.traceUsage for more information
WARNING: Dynamic loading of agents will be disallowed by default in a future release
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM warning: Sharing is only supported for boot loader classes because bootstrap classpath has been appended

Following the link at the end of the first line, it will ask you to add Mockito as an agent to your build:

<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <goals>
                <goal>properties</goal>
            </goals>
        </execution>
    </executions>
</plugin>
<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
    <configuration>
        <argLine>@{argLine} -javaagent:${org.mockito:mockito-core:jar}</argLine>
    </configuration>
</plugin>

However, the warning is still displayed.

Using:

  • Spring Boot 3.4.0
  • Apache Maven 3.9.9
  • OpenJDK 21.0.4
